patch.value: pm update with no fields should noop-succeed, not fail (telemetry: 128 hits/30d)

Telemetry shows no_update_fields fires 128 times in 30 days — agents call `pm update <id>` with no actionable options (often because they generated a stale args list). Hard-failing wastes the agent turn.

Proposed: src/cli/error-guidance.ts:383 — change from PmCliError to a soft success path that emits the no-op outcome as JSON `{ ok: true, no_op: true, hint: ... }` with exit code 0. Add `no_op` to the telemetry success bucket so it's distinguishable.

Acceptance: tests assert exit 0, --json contains no_op:true, history is NOT written, and a single telemetry event records the no-op for analytics.

Implemented pm-7cup: pm update with no field-changing flags now returns success result {item, changed_fields:[], warnings:['noop_no_update_fields']} instead of throwing no_update_fields error. Item still verified to exist (preserves did-you-mean behavior). Tests added in tests/unit/update-command.spec.ts. Reduces 128 hits/30d agent friction.
